texto: Feeling like she really was just seven or eight, Claire sat down on the floor, books all around her, and she opened the last one she’d picked up. Even though it was dark, and even though her eyes couldn’t see the words, she knew them.Knew the little prince’s story as well as her own.She closed her eyes. She leaned her head forward against the book. And she sobbed.

texto: You do what you can," he said, after seconds of silence had stretched to a minute, "to make sure your kids are safe. From the second they're born..." texto: Someone tried to kill me, and I’m going swimming. I’m going swimming, because it doesn’t matter. I’m going swimming, because I don’t matter. I’m going swimming, because that’s what Claires do. We swim and we daydream and we read and we wait for someone to care, and they never, ever, ever do.
explicacao: Citação de Jennifer Lynn Barnes sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Someone tried to kill me, and I’m going swimming. I’m going swimming, because it doesn’t matter. …” articula motivacao, care, daydream em registro de motivacao, com relevância para contextos como daydream e matter.

texto: Sometimes, having friends who were like family was a good thing, and sometimes, it was like having an endless supply of very nosy, very irritating siblings.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Jennifer Lynn Barnes: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Sometimes, having friends who were like family was a good thing, and sometimes, it was like havin…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, family, ramblings pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e ramblings.
